title: What 'par' actually means
microFact: Par assumes two putts. A par 4 = reach the green in 2 shots, then 2 putts.
lesson:
PAR is the score an expert is expected to make on a hole, and it always budgets TWO putts. So a par 3 means the expert reaches the green in one shot; a par 4 in two; a par 5 in three. Par is set mainly by length: roughly, par 3s are under ~230m, par 4s to ~450m, par 5s beyond.

A standard 18-hole course is par 70–72, usually with four par 3s, four par 5s and ten par 4s. Clover Greens, your home course, is a full 18-hole championship layout.

Here's the mindset shift that makes golf fun at your level: par is not YOUR target yet. If you shoot 110, your personal par is about a double bogey per hole (bogey golf = 90). Reframing each hole as "my par is 6" removes the despair and, ironically, lowers scores.

quiz.question: On a par 5, how many shots does par assume it takes to REACH the green?
quiz.explanation: Par always includes two putts, so a par 5 = 3 shots to reach the green + 2 putts.
